name: delete_content_range
description: Tool to delete a range of content from a google document. use when you need to remove a specific portion of text or other structural elements within a document.

tabId.description:
The ID of the tab the range is in. When omitted, the request is applied to the first tab. In a document with a single tab, if provided, it must match the singular tab's ID; if omitted, it applies to the singular tab. In a multi-tab document, if provided, it applies to the specified tab; if omitted, it applies to the first tab.
Example: "t.abcdef123456"

endIndex.description:
The zero-based end index of the range, in UTF-16 code units, relative to the beginning of the segment.
Example: 200

segmentId.description:
The ID of the header, footer, or footnote the range is in. An empty segment ID or omitting this field signifies the document's body.
Examples:
  "h.1a2b3c4d5e6f"
  ""

startIndex.description:
The zero-based start index of the range, in UTF-16 code units, relative to the beginning of the segment.
Example: 100

range.description: The range of content to delete. Deleting text across paragraph boundaries may merge paragraphs and affect styles. Certain deletions can invalidate document structure (e.g., part of a surrogate pair, last newline of critical elements, incomplete deletion of tables/equations).

document_id.description:
The ID of the document from which to delete content. This ID can be found in the URL of the Google Doc.
Example: "1aBcDeFgHiJkLmNoPqRsTuVwXyZ0123456789"
